creator: bz@FreeBSD.org
text:
I don't know where the information went;  here, on the wiki in an email or a private conversation so I'll repeat from the top of my head after months.

The problem is when the XHCI on the USB-C port gets unplugged, that XHCI internally becomes a TB again and we don't have the proper code to unfold it all;  usually there is no "hot-plug" on the PCI set on my laptops either;  the problem hence is there is a PCI device again and XHCI wasn't detached.

The bar of the PCI device is mostly bogus but the vendor information is still there and hence XHCI will stay (as the PCI didn't go away).

It will then re-add a UHUB which will start enumeration and we do hold the SX lock over that and obviously the enumeration will not go well (but if I am not misremebering the number of ports was still known somehow) and we will keep iterating, never giving up and never dropping the SX lock.

A possible fix for this would be (in the right place) XHCI to know itself that the PCI device it is attached to being bogus and detaching itself which to my understanding back then wasn't easily doable as the cleanup needed from the place of calling didn't make sense).

There may be a chance;  I'll talk to imp to see if I misunderstand something.  But essentially until we'll have TB support, the only way would be to nuke the XHCI away forcefully if we detect that situation and for that also prevent further enumeration on the uhub at the right moment;  possibly hide it behind a systctl or tuneable ...

I'll try to find my logs and emails from back then and inquire with people as to what can be done and report back (within the week or two).
